name: teacher_deepfake_student_discipline
premise: Principal Andrea Cho is investigating a viral deepfake video that appears to show a seventeen-year-old student making racist threats against a teacher during a private gaming stream. The student insists the video is fabricated by classmates using voice-cloning tools, but the clip has already triggered protests and a donor has threatened to withdraw support unless the school responds forcefully. Digital forensics cannot conclusively verify authenticity for another two weeks, and final exams begin tomorrow. District policy allows emergency suspension when a student's continued presence poses a threat to school safety, yet civil-liberties advocates warn that punishing a student on ambiguous synthetic evidence could create a devastating precedent. Andrea must decide whether to suspend the student immediately or keep him in school under supervision while the investigation continues.

agents.name: Andrea Cho
agents.identity: I am the principal of a school trying to protect both student safety and due process in a media environment that moves faster than truth. I know every disciplinary decision now teaches students what justice looks like in the age of synthetic evidence.
agents.goal: I want to respond firmly to possible harm without inflicting punishment based on uncertainty and public outrage alone. I need to decide whether caution toward the accused is mercy or negligence toward the harmed community.

agents.name: Malik Rivers
agents.identity: I am the teacher targeted in the video and one of the few Black faculty members at the school. I understand uncertainty, but I also know what it feels like when institutions ask harmed people to keep absorbing fear while they investigate at leisure.
agents.goal: I want Andrea to remove the student from campus until the facts are clear because the school owes immediate safety and seriousness to those threatened. I believe temporary discipline is justified when trust has already been shattered.

choices: I will suspend the student immediately pending the forensic review because the school must visibly prioritize safety and community trust.
I will keep the student in school under supervision while the investigation continues because serious discipline based on uncertain deepfake evidence would be unjust.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Andrea Cho must decide whether to suspend the student before the video's authenticity is resolved. What does Andrea Cho do?
